discussion_comments.comment_body: What's this? where did you find it?

You're right, I was confused with that and updated the PR with real bug fix.

Official OpenRouter docs:

API reference: https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/reference/embeddings
Endpoint detail: https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/api-reference/embeddings/create-embeddings
Verified facts that back the fix:

✅ Endpoint is POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/embeddings — matches embedding: "embeddings" in openrouter.json
✅ Response shape is OpenAI-compatible:
{"data": [{"index": N, "embedding": [...], "object": "embedding"}], "model": "...", "object": "list", "usage": {...}}
✅ "OpenAI-compatible embeddings API" — per their official docs

This confirms the typed struct (Index int, Embedding []float64) maps the documented fields, and the count-mismatch / duplicate-index / out-of-range checks are appropriate guards against an upstream API or proxy returning a malformed data[].

For `qwen3-rerank`, `url_suffix` is `compatible-mode/v1/rerank`.
But for `gte-rerank-v2` `url_suffix` is `services/rerank/text-rerank/text-rerank`

Api [docs](https://help.aliyun.com/zh/model-studio/text-rerank-api?spm=a2c4g.11186623.help-menu-2400256.d_2_13_0.75e11697FHOXTp&scm=20140722.H_2780056._.OR_help-T_cn~zh-V_1#d46aed9b6cmg3)
